prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a quantitative model that assumes the stock price follows a geometric Brownian motion, a common approach for financial asset forecasting. Historical daily adjusted close prices for COST over the past two years were used to estimate key parameters: an annualized drift (mean return) of approximately 19.88% and an annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns) of approximately 19.96%. These parameters were derived from the mean and standard deviation of daily log returns. The time horizon of 21 days was converted into a fraction of a trading year. The point estimate is the median of the projected log-normal price distribution, calculated using the current price and the estimated drift and volatility over the 21-day period. The confidence intervals were constructed by finding the corresponding quantiles of this log-normal distribution, representing the range within which the price is expected to fall with the specified probability (50%, 80%, 90%).
